title: Reading and Use of English – Part 3
description: For questions 17–24, read the text below. Use the word given in CAPITALS at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.

content.text: The (0) PRESERVATION of biodiversity is vital for the health of our planet. Healthy ecosystems provide (17) benefits, from clean air to fertile soil. However, human activities such as (18) are causing a rapid (19) of species. This (20) of natural habitats disrupts the delicate balance of nature. (21) are working tirelessly to protect (22) animals from extinction. They argue that the (23) of damaged landscapes is (24) important for our survival. By promoting (25) practices, we can ensure that future generations inherit a world rich in life. Protecting the Earth's natural heritage requires a global effort, as every species, no matter how small, plays a unique role in the intricate web of life. It is our collective responsibility to act now, before more of these invaluable resources are lost forever and the consequences become irreversible for all living beings on this fragile blue planet.

example.explanation: Noun required after the definite article; PRESERVE → PRESERVATION.

questions.explanation: Adjective required to modify 'benefits'; NUMBER → NUMEROUS.
Noun with a prefix indicating removal required; FOREST → DEFORESTATION.
Plural noun for people required; ENVIRONMENT → ENVIRONMENTALISTS.
Adjective required to describe 'animals'; DANGER → ENDANGERED.
Adverb required to modify the adjective 'important'; CRITIC → CRITICALLY.
